name: ia_procedures_pdf_demo
description: I&A procedures case modeled on Identification_and_Authentication_Procedures_USD20240212.pdf — PDF-extracted text with service identifiers (Okta, PIV, Yubikey) + vendor product names. Mirror of acct_mgmt_policy_demo but with the .pdf suffix that maps to EvidenceKind.PDF in _KIND_BY_SUFFIX — pins that suffix routing doesn't change adapter behavior.

fixture_docs.title: Identification and Authentication Procedures USD20240212
fixture_docs.text:
1.0 PURPOSE

This procedure operationalizes the Example Identification & Authentication Policy (IA-2 through IA-12 of NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5).

2.0 MULTI-FACTOR AUTHENTICATION

All interactive access to Example-managed information systems shall require multi-factor authentication consisting of:

  - Something you have: PIV card (preferred), Yubikey 5 FIPS, or DUO Mobile push
  - Something you know: a passphrase meeting the complexity rules in section 3
  - Something you are: only when PIV unavailable, via Okta Verify biometric

Federated SSO is brokered by Okta (okta.example.com); fallback for non-federated systems is local LDAP (ldap.example.local) with PAM stack enforcement.

3.0 PASSPHRASE COMPLEXITY

Minimum 15 characters; mixed case; one digit; one special character; no dictionary words; reused passphrases blocked via Specops Password Policy enforcement.

4.0 DEVICE-LEVEL

Workstations are joined to the Example Active Directory forest (example.local) and enforce conditional access via Microsoft Intune.
